content:
James Dolan told me something like this: if you take the topos of finite graphs, equip it with its double negation topology, and form the topos of sheaves on that, you get the topos of G-sets where G is the automorphism group of the Rado graph. Does this sound right, or approximately right? If so, it should be an example of a very general pattern.

(If I'm stating it in a confused way, it's my fault not his!)

I know at least another example of such phenomenon.

Caveat that I don't know the exact statement of the theorem therein, but can this be the general pattern? https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022404997001072
